testo: Some years ago I was struck by how many false things I had believed, and by how doubtful was the structure of beliefs that I had based on them. I realized that if I wanted to establish anything in the sciences that was stable and likely to last, I needed – just once in my life – to demolish everything completely and start again from the foundations.
note: USE PARADIGMATICO del programma fondazionalista classico. Cartesio dichiara esplicitamente l'intenzione di rifondazione totale (la tabula rasa che Albert diagnosticherà come 'negative genetic fallacy', albert_018). Caso archetipico per il modulo: il programma che Albert critica come patologico nel cap.1 §1.

testo: Archimedes said that if he had one firm and immovable point he could lift the world with a long enough lever; so I too can hope for great things if I manage to find just one little thing that is solid and certain.
note: USE archetipico della ricerca del 'punto di Archimede' (cit. esplicita da Albert in albert_001). Cartesio ESPLICITA la metafora che diventerà canonica nella critica fondazionalista. Il modulo deve riconoscere 'Archimedean point' come marker rosso del programma fondazionalista.

testo: But there is a supremely powerful and cunning deceiver who deliberately deceives me all the time! Even then, if he is deceiving me I undoubtedly exist: let him deceive me all he can, he will never bring it about that I am nothing while I think I am something. So after thoroughly thinking the matter through I conclude that this proposition, I am, I exist, must be true whenever I assert it or think it.
marker_linguistico: must be true whenever I assert it
note: CASO COMPLESSO E PARADIGMATICO. Il cogito è formalmente una C₂ costitutiva-apeliana (auto-confutazione performativa del dubbio universale). Cartesio la usa come fondamento → patologica per il programma (è l'arresto). Inclosura: Cartesio si tratta come esterno al sistema (T∉U) per fondare il sistema. Confronto critico con real_003 (anti-realismo) e real_004: stesso pattern, polarità diverse.

testo: I am certain that I am a thinking thing. Doesn't that tell me what it takes for me to be certain about anything? In this first item of knowledge there is simply a clear and distinct perception of what I am asserting; this wouldn't be enough to make me certain of its truth if it could ever turn out that something that I perceived so clearly and distinctly was false. So I now seem to be able to lay it down as a general rule that whatever I perceive very clearly and distinctly is true.
marker_linguistico: whatever I perceive clearly and distinctly is true
note: USE C₃ INTUIZIONISTICO PARADIGMATICO. Cartesio promuove un singolo caso (cogito) a criterio universale ('general rule'). Marker 'clear and distinct' è esattamente quello che Albert diagnosticherà in albert_007. Caso TEST: USE puro patologico di Cartesio = oggetto della DIAGNOSIS di Albert.

testo: As I see it, my understanding of what a thing is, what truth is, and what thought is, derives purely from my own nature, which means that it is innate.
marker_linguistico: derives purely from my own nature
note: USE C₃ intuizionistico nella versione 'idee innate'. Le categorie fondamentali (thing, truth, thought) sono postulate come datità non-derivabili. Tipo di arresto: appello all'innatezza come non-spiegazione.

testo: Now it is obvious by the natural light that the total cause of something must contain at least as much reality as does the effect. For where could the effect get its reality from if not from the cause? And how could the cause give reality to the effect unless it first had that reality itself?
marker_linguistico: where could it get from if not
note: USE C₃ intuizionistico: il 'natural light' come facoltà rivelativa. Cartesio postula il principio causale come evidente per intuizione, non come ipotesi. Marker 'obvious by the natural light' è canonico (analogo a 'è ovvio' del C3_dogmatico_nascosto naive ma in registro tecnico).

loc: Meditazione III idea di Dio innata
testo: By the word 'God' I understand a substance that is infinite, eternal, unchangeable, independent, supremely intelligent, supremely powerful, which created myself and anything else that may exist. The more carefully I concentrate on these attributes, the less possible it seems that any of them could have originated from me alone.
marker_linguistico: could not have originated from me alone
note: USE C₃ TEOLOGICO ARCHETIPICO. Cartesio enumera gli attributi divini canonici come datità concettuale. Polarità SELEZIONATA_DA_VALORE (come Berkeley v19, Hilbert hilb_005): il valore protetto è la metafisica cristiana. Caso simmetrico opposto a Friedman (frdm_004): qui USE non strumentale.

testo: Among my ideas, apart from the one that gives me a representation of myself, there are ideas that variously represent God. The idea of God necessarily exists. It is true that my being a substance explains my having the idea of substance; but it does not explain my having the idea of an infinite substance. That must come from some substance that is itself infinite. I am finite.
note: Argomento causale per Dio (variante dell'argomento ontologico). Cartesio infera l'esistenza di Dio dalla presenza di un'idea adeguata. USE C₃ teologico via inferenza causale 'must'. Marker 'must come from' è canonico del C3_modale_mascherato (sotto-tipo di Berkeley v19_006, Hilbert hilb_002).

loc: Meditazione III no regresso ad infinitum
testo: It is clear enough that this sequence of causes of causes can't run back to infinity, especially since I am dealing with the cause that not only produced me in the past but also preserves me at the present moment.
note: USE C₃ esplicito DI ARRESTO DEL REGRESSO. Cartesio dichiara apertamente che non si può andare ad infinito ('can't run back to infinity') — questo è ESATTAMENTE il gesto che Albert diagnostica come terzo corno (albert_003-004). Marker 'it is clear enough' è dogmatico nascosto. Caso di MASSIMA TRASPARENZA del C₃.

testo: To begin with, I see that it is impossible that God should ever deceive me. Only someone who has something wrong with him will engage in trickery or deception. That someone is able to deceive others may be a sign of his skill or power, but his wanting to deceive them is a sign of his malice or weakness; and those are not to be found in God.
marker_linguistico: impossible that God should ever deceive me
those are not to be found in God
note: USE C₃ TEOLOGICO CLASSICO. La bontà di Dio come garante della conoscenza umana — il fondamento ultimo del sistema cartesiano. Analogo strutturale di Berkeley (v19_003: Dio come 'Creatore' che chiude il regresso) e Hilbert (hilb_010: 'preestablished harmony'). Caso di USE puro non mascherato.

loc: Meditazione IV Dio garante facoltà giudizio
testo: Next, I know from experience that I have a faculty of judgment; and this, like everything else I have, was given to me by God. Since God doesn't want to deceive me, I am sure that he didn't give me a faculty of judgment that would lead me into error while I was using it correctly.
marker_linguistico: didn't give faculty that would lead into error
note: CASO STORICO DEL CIRCOLO CARTESIANO. Le idee chiare e distinte sono garantite da Dio (Meditazione IV), ma sappiamo di Dio per idee chiare e distinte (Meditazione III). USE C₂ viziosa diretta nascosta nella struttura del sistema. Marker storicamente identificato da Arnauld e Mersenne. Caso paradigmatico per il modulo: C₂ viziosa che Cartesio NON RICONOSCE.

testo: I want to pause here and spend some time contemplating God; to reflect on his attributes and to gaze with wonder and adoration on the beauty of this immense light, so far as the eye of my darkened intellect can bear it. For just as we believe through faith that the supreme happiness of the next life consists in contemplating the divine majesty, so experience tells us that this same contemplation, though much less perfect, provides the greatest joy we can have in this life.
note: Chiusura della Meditazione III in registro DEVOZIONALE-RELIGIOSO. Marker 'believe through faith' rivela che il valore protetto è la fede cristiana antecedente alla filosofia. Conferma diretta della diagnosi di cmv_007 e v19 su Berkeley: la metafisica è ingegnerizzata per preservare un valore teologico antecedente.

testo: All I need, for the purpose of rejecting all my opinions, is to find in each of them at least some reason for doubt. So I will go straight for the basic principles on which all my former beliefs rested. Whatever I have accepted until now as most true has come to me through my senses. But occasionally I have found that they have deceived me, and it is unwise to trust completely those who have deceived us even once.
note: USE del metodo del dubbio iperbolico. Strumento per arrivare al fondamento — ma il movimento è ancora interno al programma fondazionalista. Caso TEST: Cartesio sembra critico (Albert direbbe 'corretto' in superficie) ma il dubbio è solo strategia per arrivare al C₃ cogito-Dio. Polarità patologica perché il dubbio è strumentale al fondamento, non genuinamente fallibilista.

testo: The mere fact that I exist and have within me an idea of a most perfect being – that is, God – provides a clear proof that God does indeed exist. The core of the argument is this: I couldn't exist with the nature that I have – that is, containing within me the idea of God – if God didn't really exist.
marker_linguistico: idea of God proves God exists
note: USE C₂ viziosa: l'esistenza dell'idea di Dio è giustificata dall'esistenza di Dio che a sua volta è dimostrata dall'idea di Dio. Petitio principii classica. Caso PARADIGMATICO per il modulo: marker 'mere fact that...proves' è segnale di circolarità nascosta nel registro inferenziale.
